nameMeanings.name: Shaw
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): from Middle English s(c)hawe, s(c)haghe ‘small wood, grove, thicket’ (Old English sceaga). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a small wood, or habitational, for someone from any of the many places so named. Shaw and Shawe are most frequent in Lancashire and Yorkshire, where Shaw in Oldham (Lancashire) may be a principal source of the surname. The English and Lowland Scottish surname was also established in Ireland in the 17th century.
Scottish: shortened form of various surnames from the Gaelic personal name Sitheach, derived from sithech ‘wolf’.
Irish (Down and Antrim): adopted for Ó Síthigh ‘descendant of Sítheach’, a personal name based on sítheach ‘peaceful’. Compare Sheehy .

variants.name: Show
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cumberland): probably a variant of Shaw , perhaps representing a blend in Middle English of Old English sceaga and Old Norse skógr, both meaning ‘wood’.
Perhaps an Americanized form of German   Schau  .

variants.name: Sheehy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aileen, Aisling, Brigid, Declan, Egan, Eoin, Finbar, Ronan.

Irish (Limerick and Kerry): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Síthigh, ‘descendant of Sítheach’, or Mac Síthigh ‘son of Sítheach’, a form of the Irish personal name Síodhach ‘peaceful’, from the adjective sítheach ‘peaceful’ or ‘other worldly’ (see Sheehan ). The noun síth, from which this adjective derives, means basically ‘settlement’, hence both ‘peace’ and ‘place inhabited by other world beings’. This name was established in Ireland from the early 14th century, when a branch of the Scottish McDonnells, descended from Sítheach, great-grandson of Domhnaill, settled in the country.

nameMeanings.name: Johanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Johanna Shaw Lady was born in 1450, in Sauchie, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, United Kingdom as the daughter of James Shaw Sir. She had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Archibald Edmonstone 3rd of Duntreath. She died in 1490, in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Johanna Shaw Lady est née en 1450, à Sauchie, Clackmannanshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, fille de James Shaw Sir. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Archibald Edmonstone 3rd of Duntreath. Elle est décédée en 1490, à Stirling, Stirlingshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Johanna Shaw Lady wurde 1450 in Sauchie, Clackmannanshire, Schottland, United Kingdom, als Tochter von James Shaw Sir geboren. Sie hatte mit Archibald Edmonstone 3rd of Duntreath mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1490 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Johanna Shaw Lady è nata il nel 1450 nel Sauchie, Clackmannanshire, Scozia, Regno Unito come figlia di James Shaw Sir. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con Archibald Edmonstone 3rd of Duntreath. Morì nel 1490, nel Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 40 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Johanna Shaw Lady(은)는 1450년 영국 스코틀랜드 클랙먼아실르 소치에서 James Shaw Sir의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Archibald Edmonstone 3rd of Duntreath(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1490년 영국 스코틀랜드 스티를링실르 스티를링에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.
